title: lstat /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/machine.slice/libpod-SHA.scope: no such file or directory
body:
### Issue Description

Failed to run 195-run-namespaces.bats and got warning messge like this "lstat /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/machine.slice/libpod-c2455627260957820f36f657d54406a3203a6226761015f430965dc8c9d67414.scope: no such file or directory".

```shell
[root@dell-r640-076 ~]# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.8.3
API Version:  4.8.3
Go Version:   go1.21.3
Built:        Fri Jan  5 09:48:03 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[root@dell-r640-076 ~]# podman info
host:

  buildahVersion: 1.33.2

        CNI dnsname plugin

crun-1.12-1.module+el8.10.0+21077+98b84d8a.x86_64
kernel-4.18.0-535.el8.x86_64
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install podman-4.8.3-1.module+el8.10.0+21086+d8c2a7a7 and podman-test-4.8.3-1.module+el8.10.0+21086+d8c2a7a7
2. install bats
3. cd /usr/share/podman/test/system/
4. bats -t 195-run-namespaces.bats


### Describe the results you received

```shell
[root@dell-r640-076 system]# bats -t 195-run-namespaces.bats
```

### Describe the results you expected

Fix it.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release


### Additional information

It works well on crun runtime with cgroup V1.

```shell
[root@dell-r640-076 system]# bats -t 195-run-namespaces.bats
1..1
ok 1 [195] podman test all namespaces

[root@dell-r640-076 ~]# rpm -q podman podman-tests crun kernel
podman-4.8.3-1.module+el8.10.0+21086+d8c2a7a7.x86_64
podman-tests-4.8.3-1.module+el8.10.0+21086+d8c2a7a7.x86_64
crun-1.12-1.module+el8.10.0+21077+98b84d8a.x86_64
kernel-4.18.0-535.el8.x86_64
```

It seems we had similar issue 'https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11784'.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@chuanchang What is status with this on? I think for RHEL specific issues it would be better to file this via our internal Jira so it doesn't get lost upstream like it did here. Also this sounds like a runc problem and not podman if it works with crun.
